title: Iceberg: Selecting from ARRAY column with dot-separated name returns empty lists
description:
### Describe what's wrong
When querying an Iceberg table through iceberg table function or DataLakeCatalog, columns of type ARRAY<STRING> return empty lists if the column name contains a dot (.).
The same table queried via Spark returns correct values.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
Using spark:
```sql
CREATE TABLE table7 (`a.b` ARRAY<STRING>);
INSERT INTO table7 VALUES (ARRAY('a','b','c'));
```
Read with DataLakeCatalog:
```sql
SELECT *
FROM `namespace_7d8f6b8b_c93f_11f0_8e49_e0c26496f172.table7`
```
```
‚îå‚îÄa.b‚îÄ‚îê
1. ‚îÇ []  ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
```
Read with iceberg table fucntion:
```sql
SELECT *
FROM iceberg('http://minio:9000/warehouse/namespace_7d8f6b8b_c93f_11f0_8e49_e0c26496f172.db/table7/', 'admin', 'password')
```
```
‚îå‚îÄa.b‚îÄ‚îê
1. ‚îÇ []  ‚îÇ
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îÄ‚îò
1 row in set. Elapsed: 0.012 sec.
```
Read from spark:
```sql
select * from table7;
```
```
["a","b","c"]
Time taken: 0.113 seconds, Fetched 1 row(s)
```
bug_features_seed: SELECT `x_y`, z FROM table8 WHERE z = 1;
key_bug_features: ARRAY column names containing dots return empty lists
